Sweet, I can pilot the giant robot...
The expansion component was a joint purchase...
Hold on, I'm not done with it yet!
I'm the one who supplied them!
This is... the Daybreak Squadron film set!
This trip is totally worth it!
这价钱把人当傻子坑吗？
Are they trying to rip me off or what?
Three sets only, and that's final!
Is that even a mechatron anymore?
The framework's so huge, and there are way too many details...
You can call it a beanstalk!
This time I'm gonna clean out the model shop!
Please use the word "buy," thank you very much.
Wait, you watch Daybreak Squadron too?
You didn't expect that, huh? I've never missed a single episode.
Just don't come to me. I don't know anything about it, meow.
I really wanna buy it, but I'm out of credits.
你能忍受错过限量款吗？
Could you really stand to miss out on a limited edition?
Please come this way if you're a visitor!
Head to the other side if you're on a tour or sightseeing!
It's not open to visitors. Thanks for your understanding.
Not even a million-follower influencer can pull it off?
这款基于财团理念设计…
This design is based on the Corp's philosophy that...
It'll break if you touch it, so hands off.
可你已经有件一样的了…
But you already have one just like it...
Oh, that one's already been rented out.
Why won't you let me return or exchange this?
Mechatron plus cat ears? That's guaranteed to be fire.
Then paint it green, and boom, perfection!
If only I could go in and take a look...
Dream on. You can have anything in your dream.
There's something I want to ask.
尽管问吧，{NICKNAME}。为你，我永远都有时间。
Ask away, {NICKNAME}. I will always have time for you.
想了解我们这些「无名客」的事情？可以哦，我尽量长话短说。
You want to understand more about the Nameless? Sure, I'll keep it short.
我？哎呀哎呀，我没有什么有趣的过往呢……
Me? Ahhh, I don't have an interesting past...
在我出生以前很久很久，星穹列车就坠落在我的星球上了。我在少时发现了它，从那时起，我的生活就与它紧紧联系在了一起。
Long before I was born, the Astral Express crashed onto my world. I found it when I was still young. From then on, my life became closely bound to it.
我花了很长时间修复了列车，搭乘它启航前往群星……这是列车的心愿，也是我的梦想：见识未知的星辰海洋。
It took me ages to repair the Express and board it to travel to the stars... It was the wish of the Express and my dream to set sail through the unknown sea of stars.
他呀，是个老好人。外表像个热心肠的大叔，骨子里还是个意气风发的少年郎——别告诉他是我说的。
Welt? He may appear to be a kind old man, but deep inside he's still a strapping youth with a hopeful heart. Don't tell him I said that though.
刚遇见瓦尔特的时候，他和同伴在太空里漂流，列车把他俩捞了上来。
When we first met Welt, he and his friend were drifting through space. The Express saved the two of them.
瓦尔特来自一个我没听说过的星球，他现在还在寻找回去的办法。不过，我总觉得他对冒险更有兴趣，现在他已经不怎么提回家的话题了。

Welt is still trying to find a way back home to a planet that I've never heard of. However, it seems he's more interested in going on adventures. It's been a while since I've heard him mention going home.
没有丹恒的许可，我不能随便说他的事情；好像有什么敌人在追逐他，丹恒来到星穹列车是个巧合，留下则是因为无处可去。

It wouldn't be right of me to talk about Dan Heng without his permission, but it seems he has enemies after him. He boarded the Astral Express by coincidence, and stayed because he had nowhere else to go.
现在他是车上的智库维护者和生物学家，但很奇怪，他那么喜欢读书，却不怎么愿意和黑塔女士交流。
Now he is the biologist and overseer of the data bank on the Express. It's strange... With how much he likes to read, I'm surprised he doesn't like to talk with Madam Herta.
更多的事你得自己去问他啦。
If you want to know more, ask him yourself.
别看小三月总是嘻嘻哈哈的，她的内心其实很不安。
Despite her cheerful demeanor, March is very anxious inside.
列车发现她的时候，她被封在一块巨大的六相冰里…瓦尔特好容易才把她带回车厢，没过多久，六相冰就在我们眼前融化，消失了。

When the Express found her, she was sealed in an enormous piece of Six-Phased Ice... It took Welt a lot of work to get her back to the Express. Not long after, the Six-Phased Ice melted and vanished before our eyes.
小三月醒来后，什么都不记得。这架列车上的其他人至少有一个可以称为家乡的地方，只有她是真的无依无靠，离开列车就无处可去。

March couldn't remember anything when she woke up. The people on the Express have at least a place to call home, but that's not the case for her. She doesn't have anywhere to return to outside of the Express.
最近她变得越来越积极了，可我觉得，那孩子只是努力用表面的快乐遮挡实质的恐惧呢……
Lately, she's been getting more proactive, but I believe she's only trying to cover up her fear with a superficial happiness...
你想了解这辆列车？我很高兴，它是我们很重要的同伴。
Ohh, you want to know more about the Express? I'm glad. After all, it's an important companion of ours.
About the history of the Express...
星穹列车是「开拓」阿基维利制造的工具，用于承载祂与无名客进行星际旅行。据传这样的载具不止一台，但列车里没有其它的纪录。

The Astral Express was a tool created by Akivili the Trailblaze, who used it to transport THEMSELF and the Nameless across the galaxy. It is rumored that there are other vehicles like it, but the Express has no such records.
我发现列车时，它的记忆体已经损坏，丢失了很多宝贵的资料。只知道：列车确实是阿基维利的造物；列车确实曾与阿基维利一同旅行。

When I found the Express, its memory had been severely damaged, with much of its valuable information lost. All I know is that the Express is an Aspect of Creation built by Akivili themself, and used to travel the cosmos.
至于它的制造过程，和停留的原因，都已不得而知了。
As for how it was built and how it was damaged, I do not have an answer.
About where the Express is headed...
列车正沿着「开拓」曾经的旅线行驶。部分站点的名称因记忆体的损坏而丢失了，但首站和末站的名字都是「裴迦纳」——阿基维利的故乡。
The Express is traveling on a route that Trailblaze once embarked on. The names of some destinations have been lost, but the first and final stops were both at Pegana — Akivili's home world.
我们推测星穹列车是从裴迦纳出发，沿途停靠，并最终回到起点，再次下一段旅程。但是由于星核的出现，每一站都发生了延误。

We speculate the Astral Express started its journey from Pegana, stopping at each destination along the way before returning there for its next journey. However, the appearance of the Stellarons has caused a delay at each stop.
About the Express' source of power...
银河里流传着一个说法：列车的核心是星神阿基维利的心脏，它提供了星穹列车穿越诸界的能力。但我在列车上没有找到对应的证据。

There is a legend in the galaxy: The heart of Akivili themself lies in the core of the Astral Express, providing it with the power to travel between worlds. But I found no evidence of that aboard the Express.
何况，「开拓」尚未陨落时列车就存在了，总不能有两颗心脏对吧？但，这辆列车很可能拥有某种机制，使它可以从开拓命途搬运力量。

Besides, the Express existed before the Trailblaze fell. There's no way they could have had two hearts, right? However, it is likely that the Express possesses some sort of mechanism to transfer power from the Path of Trailblaze.
普通的命途行者可做不到这一点。
It wouldn't be possible with a normal Pathstrider.
陨落的星神，逝去的「开拓」——祂的离开是一个谜，但在所有已知的星神里，阿基维利是最接近人类的一位。
The fallen Aeon, deceased Trailblaze — THEIR passing is still a mystery, but of all the known Aeons, Akivili was the closest to mankind.
在列车智库的纪录里，祂和凡人一同行走，一同冒险，一同战斗，一同欢庆。作为被「原动力」束缚的神明，阿基维利拥有几近凡人的自由。

In the databank aboard the Express, it is recorded that THEY walked among the mortals — adventuring, fighting, and celebrating with THEM. Although THEY were an Aeon restrained by the "Primum Mobile," Akivili enjoyed a freedom similar to us mortals.
祂是如此与众不同……祂的陨落也突如其来，传说某位星神杀死了祂，可是我不相信。
THEY were different from most... But THEIR passing came so suddenly that it was thought THEY were killed by another Aeon. I don't believe that to be the case.
银河是很广大的啊。你突然这么问我，我也不知从何说起……
The galaxy is endlessly vast. I wouldn't know where to begin, especially if you ask me that so suddenly...
即使去过了那么多星球，我对银河还是所知甚少，因为它太广袤啦。关于银河的实质众说纷纭，倒是有几个假说可以和你分享。
I've been to many different worlds, yet I still know very little about the galaxy, simply because it's too vast. As for its nature, there are a few theories that I can share with you.
拥趸最多的是「宇宙之树」说。提出者是「智识」的令使：天才俱乐部第一位会员赞达尔。他认为银河是一棵虚数巨木，被阻隔的星系则是一片片树叶。

The most popular is probably the Cosmos Tree theory, proposed by Zandar, Emanator of Erudition and the first member of the Genius Society. He compared the galaxy to an enormous imaginary tree, with its leaves being individual universes.
因此，只有直接从虚数中获取能量的星神以及被星神赐福的令使能够穿过被虚数能量填充的空域，进行星际旅行。也因此，每个诞生文明的星球才如此相似。

Therefore, only Aeons, who draw their energy from the imaginary, and Emanators, who are blessed by Aeons, can travel through the spaces filled with imaginary energy. That's why planets where civilizations exist are so similar.
但这个假说也存在很多问题。俱乐部<unbreak>56</unbreak>#会员以利亚萨拉斯发明了超距遥感，证伪了虚数的唯一性，一下子动摇了宇宙之树说的根基。

The theory has its flaws. Elias Salas, the <unbreak>56</unbreak>th member of the Genius Society, invented ultraremote sensing, proving that the Imaginary element is unique. This shook the foundation of the "Cosmos Tree" theory.
除此之外还有很多假说：「伸缩」论，「热炬」论，「平行成像」论……等等。「谜语人」派系宣称银河只是一场梦境，据我所知，Ⅸ的崇拜者很喜欢这个说法。

There are other theories as well: The "Stretching" theory, the "Heat Torch" theory, and the "Parallel Imaging" theory... The Riddlers claim the galaxy is just a dream and IX's followers seem to like that claim.
星神是银河里最神秘的存在。我们只知道祂是从智慧生物升格而成，但条件是什么，原理是什么，就连俱乐部的天才们也一无所知。

Aeons are the most mysterious beings in the galaxy. All we know is that THEY ascended from the form of intelligent beings. As for the how and why, even the geniuses over at the Genius Society haven't the slightest clue.
当一个生物跃升成为星神后，祂就会拥有「命途」的执掌权；祂可以任意搬运该命途中的虚数能量，同时也被「原动力」所束缚。
Upon ascending to Aeon-hood, that being gains power over the Paths, free to choose the allocation of imaginary energy however THEY wish, while suffering the restrictions of the "Primum Mobile."
「毁灭」的星神只有湮灭宇宙的念头，「智识」的星神一心求解万物的答案；「存护」的星神无休止地铸造着墙壁，「神秘」的星神致力于让确凿无疑的信息变得混沌不清……

The Aeon of Destruction seeks only to destroy the universe, while the Aeon of Erudition wants to find the answer for all that exists. Meanwhile, the Aeon of Preservation continues to forge walls, and the Aeon of Enigmata devotes themself to obscuring all that is known...
星神身上的谜团实在太多了，据说黑塔女士拉上了几位会员，正想办法攻克祂们的未解之谜。
A cloud of mystery shrouds the Aeons. I heard Madam Herta recruited a team to try and solve the mysteries about THEM.
相较于星神，派系就要好懂很多了：一群凡人为了共同的目的聚集在一起，践行自身对「星神」和「命途」的理解，即是派系。
Compared to the Aeons, the factions are much easier to understand. Mortals with the same objective gather together to practice their understanding of Aeons and the Paths.
星神大多遥不可及，派系却和我们密切相关；阿基维利开拓银河之后，人们开始意识到不同世界的存在，于是越来越多的人们试图利用星神之力进行星际旅行。

Many Aeons are unreachable, but the factions are close to us. After Akivili trailblazed across the galaxy, people became aware of the existence of other worlds. Gradually, more people started trying to use the power of the Aeons to travel between worlds.
比如「星际和平公司」：他们崇拜「存护」克里珀，却阴错阳差成了银河最大的经济体；
The Interastral Peace Corporation is a good example: They worship Qlipoth, the Aeon of Preservation, but somehow became the largest economic entity in the galaxy.
又比如「天才俱乐部」：他们在「智识」的护佑下致力于科研，但其中也不乏黑塔女士那样的怪人。
Another example is the Genius Society: There is no shortage of eccentrics like Madam Herta who dedicate themselves to scientific research under the protection of Erudition.
这些派系拥有和我们一样的星际跃迁能力。想要在银河中顺利旅行，很难不与他们打交道。
These factions possess the same power as us to voyage between worlds. It would be hard to travel through the galaxy without them.
每位星神的诞生都意味着一道「命途」的开启；命途的本质仍是个谜，我们只好用凡人能理解的方式进行类比：就是某种哲学「概念」。
The birth of an Aeon gives rise to a Path. The nature of the Paths remains a mystery, leaving us to draw an analogy in a way that mortals can understand: It's a philosophical concept of sorts.
当一个人的意志与命途发生重叠时，就可视为其行走在命途之上；如果他的意念足够强大，便可从命途中汲取少许力量。这样的人被称作「命途行者」。

A person is considered to be on a Path when their will overlaps with that Path. If the person has a strong enough will, they can draw power from that Path. Those who can do so are called Pathstriders.
命途行者的力量超越凡人，但仍很弱小；比起完全执掌命途的星神，就像在汪洋大海中的一滴水一样。偶尔，星神会将命途的力量直接赐给凡人，让他们成为「令使」。

Pathstriders possess extraordinary power, but are still insignificant compared to the Aeons, like a drop of water in a vast ocean. Sometimes, Aeons will bestow a mortal with their power, making them an "Emanator" of that Aeon.
顺带一提，命途一旦开启，就无法关闭。即使执掌的星神陨落了，命途仍然开放。这就是我们在阿基维利陨落后仍能穿越星际的原因。
I should mention that once a Path is open, it cannot be closed, even with the fall of its Aeon. That is how we are still able to travel across the stars, despite Akivili's passing.
「开拓」是我们的使命，也是列车在银河间往来的力量之来源。
"Trailblaze" is our mission and the source of strength that powers the Express to travel across the galaxy.
「探索」未知的世界，不断拓宽前方的旅道。
Explore the unknown world to continue our journey ahead.
「了解」当地的人文生态，融入其中。
Understand the local culture and immerse ourselves within it.
「建立」与新世界之间的联系，为其喜乐，共其忧虑。
Establish a connection with the new world — rejoice with it and share in its fears.
「连结」世界与世界，踏出永不断绝的道路。
Connect worlds together, carving an endless path.
I don't have any more questions.
路上如果遇到了有趣的事情，不妨把它们记下来吧。以后会成为有趣的谈资喔。
You should record anything interesting you come across during your adventures. In the end, they might just become important points of conversation.
There's something I want to ask.
你想了解什么？我会尽力为你说明。
What do you want to know? I will do my best to answer your questions.
列车组的大家吗？好啊，你想问谁？
About everyone on the Express? Who would you like to know about?
哈哈，她是这辆列车的主人——虽说我们管帕姆叫列车长，但大家都知道，姬子才是星穹列车的所有者。
Haha, she's the owner of the Express. We joke around calling Pom-Pom the conductor, but everyone knows Himeko is the boss.
一切的故事都从她遇见星穹列车开始，从那以后，他们几乎没有分开过。
It all started with her encounter with the Astral Express, and they haven't been apart since then.
她是那种…极具热情的人；她的热情就像一颗剧烈燃烧的太阳。多数时候，她很神秘；但偶尔，你会觉得她在燃烧自己，朝着梦想飞驰而去。
She's... extremely passionate, like a burning sun. However, she remains mysterious most of the time. Once in a while, you feel that she's burning herself out trying to accomplish her dream.
只有像她那样的人才配得上星穹列车——我是这么想的——姬子一定有个非常重要的梦想，构成了她整个人生的愿景。
Only someone like her is worthy of the Astral Express. I think Himeko's vision of her whole life revolves around... a very important dream.
说实话，我不记得帕姆什么时候出现了…好像在我来到列车之前？不，好像之后才出现的…奇怪。
To be honest, I don't know when Pom-Pom appeared... I think it was before I came to the Express? No wait, maybe it was after...
帕姆就像星穹列车上的小精灵。不管什么时候，只要列车上的人需要帮助，它都会立刻出现。但可不要因此而小觑它。
Pom-Pom is like the spirit of the Astral Express. Whenever anyone on the Express needs help, they will appear immediately. It would be ill-advised to underestimate them.
帕姆生气的模样可是非常可怕的…对，非常可怕……
Pom-Pom is terrifying when they get angry... Yes, terrifying...
丹恒是个孤独的孩子。他看着冷冰冰的，但内心不坏，可能是长期的逃亡生活把他塑造成现在这个模样，有时他会让我想起自己年轻的时候……

Dan Heng is a lonely child. He may appear distant and cold, but his heart is kind. Perhaps, he's the way he is today because he spent so much time on the run. Sometimes, he reminds me of myself when I was young...
我们不知道他在逃避什么。有一次他跟我说：他也不知道，只是有什么在追逐他，让他不得不逃亡；他曾经待过仙舟，有阵子为公司工作。
We don't know what he's running from. He once told me that he didn't know either. All he knew was that something was chasing him and that he had to run away — first from the Xianzhou, then the IPC.
但是直觉警告他，要他快走，于是他搭上「悲悼伶人」的船，逃离了公司，几经辗转来到列车。这是他待得最久的地方。
So he boarded the ship of a troupe called the "Mourning Actors" and escaped the IPC. After a while he made his way to the Express, and he's stayed here longer than anywhere else.
不管是谁想要伤害丹恒，我们是不会答应的。丹恒是列车组的一员，敢招惹星穹列车的人，就要准备好承受我和姬子的怒火。
Don't worry, no matter who or what wants to hurt Dan Heng, we won't let them. Those who dare attack members of the Astral Express should be prepared to suffer the wrath of me and Himeko.
小三月的事情你听姬子说过了吗？她被封在一块宇宙浮冰里，我们无意中发现，才把她救了出来。
Did Himeko tell you about March 7th? She was trapped in ice, floating through space. We happened upon her and rescued her.
那是一种非常特殊的冰块，叫做「六相冰」，是遵循虚数法则的造物，不会因外界干扰而转变形态。
It was a unique type of ice known as Six-Phased Ice — a substance that adheres to Imaginary Law, meaning that external forces cannot change its form.
把她封进六相冰里的人…不论是谁…要么是为了保护小三月，要么就是为了放逐她。我觉得，那块冰在宇宙中漂流的时间应该很久了。
Whoever sealed her inside the Six-Phased Ice... No matter who it is... did so either to protect or banish her. I believe she had been floating through space for some time.
我的过去？要让你失望了，我只是个普通上班族。
My past? Sorry to disappoint you, but I'm just an ordinary white-collar employee.
在我的故乡，我在一家动画工作室担任原画设计师。
Back home, I worked as a concept artist at an animation studio.
这有些说来话长了…我的力量来自一位前辈，可以说我继承的是他的使命，和他的意志。
Long story short, my powers come from an older acquaintance. You can say I inherited his mission and will.
我可以把理解原理的事物「拟态」为现实，这个能力在工作的时候还挺好用的。至于黑洞…那说来话更长了。
I can mimic anything as long as I understand its principles, which is useful for work. As for black holes... that's an even longer story.
有机会再慢慢跟你说吧。
I'll tell you about it some other time.
我的伙伴们在很远的地方——故乡…借助星穹列车，我也寻找着回去的办法。毕竟那里还有人在等我。
My friends are far away... I hope to find a way back home with the Astral Express' help. People are waiting for me to go back, after all.
但这并不是心急就能解决的问题，能在各世界往来的办法并不多，而星穹列车慷慨接纳了我。
But this isn't something that can be solved with impatience. There aren't many ways to travel between worlds, and the Astral Express has graciously accepted me.
我也托黑塔女士发出了讯号，如果我的家人能收到的话，他们会知道我平安无事的。
I've asked Madam Herta to send a signal to my home. If my family receives my message, they will know that I am safe.
现在，我的伙伴就是这架列车和大家——还有你。
Now, my friends are the Express, the Crew... and you, of course.
敌人吗？曾经的敌人已经被打败了。
Enemies? I've taken care of my past enemies.
要说的话，我如今的敌人就是纳努克、星核与反物质军团吧。
If I have to say, my current enemies are Nanook, the Stellaron, and the Antimatter Legion.
我要保护这个世界的美好，不被「毁灭」所破坏。
I want to protect the beauty of this world from those that follow the Path of Destruction.
About the history of the galaxy...
银河的历史有太多空缺之处了，它的长度远超人类的认知极限。何况，还有星神「神秘」四处破坏珍贵的历史纪录。
There are many gaps in the history of the galaxy, which extend far beyond the limits of human cognition. Moreover, the Aeon Enigmata is going around destroying precious historical records everywhere.
据说宇宙自诞生至星神出现前，还有一段巨大的空白；在那段空白里游弋着名为「古兽」的奇妙存在。
It is said that there was a huge gap in the records between the birth of the universe and when Aeons appeared. During that time, strange existences known as Leviathans roamed.
但星神诞生之后，古兽就消失了。传说「贪饕」奥博洛斯是最后一只留存于世的古兽，不知是真是假。
However, Leviathans disappeared when the Aeons were born. Legend has it that Oroboros the Voracity was the last remaining Leviathan, but no one knows if that's true.
最古老的星神克里珀催生了「星际和平公司」，从公司诞生起，我们才有了现代意义上的银河史，在那之前的记录完全不成系统。
The most ancient Aeon, Qlipoth, gave birth to the Interastral Peace Corporation. From then on, we have the modern history of the galaxy — but before then, the records are incomplete.
噢，我喜欢这个话题！说真的，星神是什么呢？
Oh, I love this topic! Honestly, what are Aeons?
我一度将故乡的敌人与星神混淆，但很快发现两者本质并不相同；反物质军团的一名大君就能燃烧星系，手下的浩劫先锋单枪匹马足以灭星。

I once confused the enemy of my home with the Aeons, but I soon discovered the two are different in nature. An Overlord in the Antimatter Legion can incinerate entire star systems, while their Calamity Vanguards are perfectly capable of destroying a planet by themselves.
星神们强大几乎无敌，早在「毁灭」之前，就有「繁育」的寰宇虫灾席卷群星；「丰饶」与「巡猎」的争斗不知令多少世界生灵涂炭。

Aeons are so powerful that they're near invincible. Long before the Destruction existed, a swarm disaster from the Propagation spread through the stars, and the war between the Abundance and the Hunt has also ruined many worlds.
好在至少有一条法则确凿无疑：星神能且只能依照其「原动力」的内核行动，我们永远也不用担心「同谐」掀起颠覆银河的风暴。
At least one rule is certain: Aeons can only operate according to their Primum Mobile. For example, we won't ever have to worry about the Harmony stirring up a storm.
派系的概念也许大了些。按照「为了共同的目的聚集在一起」的标准，我们星穹列车也可以说是一个派系啊。
The concept of factions might be too big. If a faction is all about "coming together for a common purpose," then even the Astral Express can be considered as one.
银河中围绕着诸星神，有近百个派系。有的忠实践行星神的原动力，有的却把命途曲解到滑稽可笑的地步了。
There are almost a hundred factions involved with the Aeons. Some faithfully follow the Primum Mobile of the Aeons, while others have twisted the concept of the Paths to the point of absurdity.
其实，作为历史爱好者，比起星神我更喜欢研究派系的诞生、发展与变革，因为它体现的是活生生的人类为了信仰和利益而走出的道途。

As a history buff, I would prefer to study the birth, development, and transformation of the factions rather than the Aeons. The factions represent the roads human beings chose out of faith and benefit.
比如「存护」克里珀的信徒是怎么演变成「星际和平公司」的？好好想想吧，你会发现这件事充满了黑色幽默。
For example, how did the followers of Qlipoth the Preservation become the Interastral Peace Corporation? If you think about it, you'll discover it's full of dark humor.
命途的实质还有待研究。现阶段，我们只能以「哲学概念」这种浅显易懂的形式来形容它。
We still need to study the nature of the Paths. For now, we can only describe it as a "philosophical concept."
它是虚数的一部分，是形而上的精神聚合体。当满足某个条件之后，这部分虚数能就会被某个智慧生物完全占据。
It is a part of the Imaginary element, the metaphysical aggregation of the spirit. When specific conditions are met, an intelligent being will completely take over this part of the Imaginary.
该智慧生物被称为星神，祂拥有的能量被称为命途。星神可以任意地使用命途中的能量，同时也被牢牢束缚在命途之中。
These intelligent beings are called Aeons and the energy THEY possess is called Paths. Aeons are free to use THEIR Paths' energy however THEY please, but are also bound by it.
I don't have any more questions.
We narrowly averted disaster, and now we're home.
We almost didn't make it back...
哎呀，看来这个世界也是危险重重；打从星穹列车启航，每个被星核污染堵塞的世界，都是这么麻烦。
Hah, it seems this world is a dangerous place, too. Ever since the Astral Express embarked on this journey, every world contaminated by the Stellaron has been problematic.
在你们三个上车之前，我、瓦尔特…还有某个家伙一起旅行。那时也是这样，一点点地开拓新世界，接触星核，打通道路……

Before the three of you boarded the Express, Welt, I... and someone else were adventuring together. It was the same back then as well — we would slowly trailblaze new worlds, find the Stellaron, and clear a path...
沟通引发的误会，文化差异造成的敌意，以及心怀不轨的敌人。困难不外乎这几种，但总会有点新花样。

No matter what world we were on, some difficulties remain constant — misunderstandings caused by communication, enmity caused by cultural differences, and enemies with malicious intentions. But there is always something new.
从来没听起你提过这些…
You've never mentioned these things before...
好想听你讲讲过去的事情…
I want to hear about your past...
你还有正事要干，我呢，也有列车停靠时维护检修之类的细碎工作要干呀。
You've still got work to do. As for me, I've got to maintain the Express while it is parked.
等这个世界的事情了结，我们可以休息一阵子。你要是仍然有兴趣听，再来找我吧。
We can take a long break after we're done with this world. Come find me if you're still interested then.
We narrowly averted disaster, and now we're home.
We almost didn't make it back...
真可惜，这次没法和你们一起登陆……
It's a shame I couldn't go with you...
{NICKNAME}, you're welcome to come and talk to me any time.
这样就算不能和你们同行，至少能分享你们的冒险经历。
Although I cannot travel with you, I can at least share your adventures.
What's up, {NICKNAME}? Back on the Express slacking off, huh?
我是专程来听你讲故事的…
I'm here to listen to your stories...
暂时闲了下来，想听你讲讲过去…
I'm free for now and want to hear you talk about the past...
喔，差点都给忘了…那么，长话短说，该从哪里开始讲起呢？
Oh, I almost forgot about that... Then, to make a long story short, where should I begin?
姬子小姐是什么时候登上列车的？
Himeko, when did you first board the Express?
这个问题可把我难倒了……
七年、八年…不，都不对。抱歉了，我不像丹恒，从来没有在列车上记录行车日志的习惯。
Seven years, eight years... No, that's not right. I'm sorry. I'm not Dan Heng — I've never kept a diary aboard the Express.
列车在不同世界间行驶，时间的流动也变得模糊不清了呢……
The Express traverses through different worlds, and the flow of time can become blurry...
不过，说起我和列车缘分的开始，我很清楚记得，那天正巧是我的生日。当时，我在故乡的野外游荡，打算找个不受光污染干扰的地方观察「天堂之指」的亮度与运动轨迹。

But I'm clear about the beginning of my story with the Express. That day just so happened to be my birthday. Back then, I was hiking outdoors and looking for a place without any light pollution to observe the light and orbit of Heaven's Finger.
随后我就在镜头里发现一个亮斑划过天空。我本以为那是一颗流星，没什么特别的。
Then, using a telescope, I saw a bright spot streak across the sky. I thought it was a comet and thought nothing of it.
但直到亮斑越变越大，我意识到有些不对劲……那东西径直穿过大气层，没有被烧尽，而是冲我所在的地方飞了过来。
But it wasn't until the spot grew bigger and bigger that I realized there was something wrong... That thing pierced straight through the atmosphere without burning up, and flew straight at me.
于是我拼命驱车逃跑，直到听到身后震天巨响，仿佛整个世界都翻了个面……
So I drove as fast as I could to get out of there, until I heard a loud crash behind me as if the entire world had been flipped upside down...
我在一个巨大的陨坑里看到了这辆半截埋入土里的列车。当时我就对自己说，这根本就不是我们世界能造出的东西，靠近观察也太危险了……
I saw a train half-buried in a huge crater. When it happened, I said to myself that this cannot possibly be a thing of this world. It's too dangerous to get close to it...
但是出于人类基本的好奇心，我还是壮着胆子走近看了一眼。我心里想，一眼，就看一眼。
But, out of basic human curiosity, I dared to take a closer look. I keep thinking to myself, just one look, just one look.
于是我想办法进到了列车里面，列车的内部结构实在是叹为观止，尤其是它的引擎……
So, I figured out a way to go inside the Express. The internal structure was incredible, especially its engine...
等回过神来的时候，我已经充满干劲地把列车修好，和它一起踏上旅程了。
By the time I came to my senses, I had already passionately finished fixing the Express and was already embarking on a journey aboard it.
What kind of wild plot is this?
这根本是被精神控制了吧？
人生有许多发展本就是出乎意料，不受控制的。就像你来到列车的经历一样。
Many developments in life are inherently unexpected or uncontrollable, just like your experience with the Express.
你和瓦尔特先生什么时候认识的？
When did you and Mr. Yang meet?
关于，这个问题…唔…瓦尔特是什么时候来的？
About that... Um... When did Welt get here?
总之，是在多年以前，当时我和帕姆受黑塔小姐的委托，去往被遗弃的废墟世界萨尔索图寻找一种被称为「回音玻璃」的材料……
Many years ago, Pom-Pom and I were tasked by Herta to go to the abandoned and ruined world of Salsotto, looking for a special material called Cadence Glass...
就在列车跃迁结束后，我们收到了某个不明信号。信号的来源是一座巨大的星舰残骸。
Just as the Express finished its warp jump, we picked up an unknown signal. It was coming from the wreckage of a huge starship.
按照《列车安全手册》的标准，对于明显危险的信号来源应当不予理睬。因为你不知道发信的一方究竟是落难者还是准备好陷阱的强盗……

Based on the Express Safety Manual, such an obviously dangerous signal should be ignored. You have no idea whether the party sending the signal is a victim needing rescue, or a bandit lurking behind a trap...
但是出于人类基本的好奇心，我们打算过去瞧一眼，就立刻发车前往萨尔索图办正事。
But out of basic human curiosity, we decided to take a look and immediately set out for Salsotto to do our business.
在那之后，瓦尔特就和我们一起旅行了。
Welt joined up with us after that.
等等…过程省略的也太多了吧？
Wait... You're missing lots of details, aren't you?
这当中到底发生了什么啊？
没有什么曲折的救援过程，只是正常程序罢了，列车上也多了两个乘客。
There was no torturous rescue process, just the normal procedures. In the end, there were two new passengers on the Express.
为了报答列车的救命之恩，并支付救援相关的费用，他们选择留在列车上打工抵债。
They chose to work for the Express as a way to say thanks for saving their lives and pay off their rescue-related expenses.
怎么样？不知道说了这些，有没有满足你的好奇心呢？
How about that? Is that good enough to satisfy your curiosities?
不管你有没有满足，要是你还有别的问题，可以去问问瓦尔特。{NICKNAME}，现在我得继续干活了。
Well, satisfied or not, you can ask Welt if you have any more questions. If you'll excuse me, {NICKNAME}, I have to get on my work now.
你也可以去找瓦尔特聊聊，如果他有空的话……
You can go and chat with Welt if he has the time...
列车外壳在跃迁过程中的损坏情况……
The damage to the exterior of the Express during the warp jump...
嗯，{NICKNAME}，你回来了啊。有什么特别的事情要分享给我听听吗？
Mmm, {NICKNAME}. You're back. Anything special you've got to share with me?
躲避寒潮最后的堡垒，城市维持着恒温的奇迹……
The last bastion against the Eternal Freeze, and the miracle of a city that manages to maintain a constant temperature...
裂界中长出的奇怪结晶，对人体有侵蚀危害……
Weird crystals grown out of the Fragmentum, which can corrode and hurt humans...
具有智能的古代机器人……
在这样一颗被冰雪覆盖，灭绝生机的世界里，还有这么多值得探索的东西。如果不是亲身经历，确实很难想象。
There's still so much to explore in this snow-covered extinct world. If you didn't see it for yourself, it'd be really hard to imagine.
瓦尔特先生似乎很渴望冒险…
Mr. Yang seems to crave adventure...
在列车上一定很无聊吧？
Is it boring aboard the Express?
不怕你笑话，我曾经安然过了八年这样「无聊」的生活。两点一线，朝九晚五，一度甚至以为自己可以永远这样生活下去。

I don't care if you laugh, but I once lived eight boring years like this. Just a straight line from home to work and back again, working from nine to five. I once thought I could live that way forever.
不过和你一样，因为某些原因我最终还是离开了故乡，登上了这辆列车。随着旅程展开，过去战斗时热血沸腾的感觉也渐渐回来了……
But, just like you, I left my home for my own reasons and boarded this Express. As the journey unfolds, that blood-boiling feeling from the heat of the battle gradually returned to me...
瓦尔特先生来自什么样的世界？
What kind of world did Mr. Yang come from?
那是一个很美丽的世界，四季分明，有壮丽的山脉与海洋，是一颗宜居星球。
It's a beautiful planet, with distinct seasons, splendid mountains and oceans, and a habitable atmosphere.
然而，那颗星球却被某种名为「崩坏」的力量困扰着，不断上演毁灭和兴盛的轮回……
But it's also a planet haunted by a power known as the Honkai, constantly playing out cycles of destruction and prosperity...
瓦尔特先生过去是做什么的？
What was your profession, Mr. Yang?
你的表情…怎么，有这么令人意外吗？
Your expression... What? Is it really that surprising?
在和平年代拥有一份自己喜欢的职业，这可是许多人梦寐以求的生活。
Having a career you love in a peaceful world is the dream of many people.
瓦尔特先生为什么会离开故乡？
Why did you leave your home?
也不是什么特别的理由……
为了对抗天外降临的外星入侵者，我和某个…老朋友一起踏上旅途，想要一劳永逸地解决这个威胁。
Just to fight alien invaders from beyond the sky. An... old friend and I embarked on a journey to end that threat once and for all.
我当时要对付的敌人，是某个被称为「天上之人」的异星种族。
The invaders I was dealing with was an alien race known as "the ones above."
要知道，星辰间遍布着许多文明。他们中有的危险程度丝毫不逊于反物质军团。
You know, there are many civilizations scattered throughout the stars. Some are no less dangerous than the Antimatter Legion.
只要旅行的时间够久，你一定会遇上其中一两个种族。到时候你就会明白，很多事情并非靠和平交涉就能解决的。
Travel for long enough, and you'll be sure to bump into an alien species or two. That's when you'll understand many things cannot be resolved through peaceful negotiations.
唯一能保护你关心的人的方法，就是与之战斗。
The only way to guarantee complete protection of someone you care about is through combat.
老朋友…似乎听姬子提起过？
This old friend... I think Himeko mentioned them before.
哦？姬子竟然还会提到那家伙，看来她也不是那么生气嘛…
Hmm? Himeko did? Looks like she's not that mad, then...
这位朋友如今已经离开列车，希望他能在旅途中找到自己想要寻找的东西。
This old friend had already left the Express. I hope he can find what he is looking for on his journey.
瓦尔特先生，请前往星轨稳定系统附近完成检查……
Mr. Yang, please go to the star rail stabilization system and complete inspections...
{NICKNAME}，列车长有安排，我得干活去了。
{NICKNAME}, the conductor has plans for me. I have to go to work.
希望你们接下来的任务能一切顺利。对了，谢谢你能和我分享这一路的冒险。
Hope your mission goes smoothly. By the way, thank you for sharing all your adventures with me.
真抱歉，帕姆又在催我干活了。
I'm so sorry, but Pom-Pom is cracking the whip again.
如果时间允许，希望你能多多和我聊一聊你们在地表的冒险。
If time permits, I hope you can chat with me more about your adventures on that planet.
哔嘟…「家用寻物仪」查宝为你服务。请使用语音输入目标物件关键词。
Beep, beep, beep... Findie the Home-Use Object Finder at your service. Please say the target object you want to search for.
哔嘟…检索到近似目标物件，开始进入搜索模式。请根据本机指引进行搜索。
Beep, beep, beep... Potential target object detected, search mode activated. Please commence search according to my instructions.
哔嘟…寻物探针间距离过于接近，无法进行准确定位。请重新选择位置。
Beep, beep, beep... Distance between the finder probes is too close for precise location detection. Please reselect position.
哔嘟…即将为您播放「家用寻物仪安全使用指南」，请跟读并背诵全文……

哔嘟…多看「家用寻物仪安全使用指南」，每次都有新发现。
Beep, beep, beep... Keep reading the Home-Use Object Finder Safety Manual. You'll learn something new every time.
哔嘟！哔嘟！发现近似目标物件。本次搜索已结束，即将进入休眠模式……
Beep, beep, beep! Potential target object detected. The current search has concluded, entering sleep mode...

Beep, beep, beep... Interastral Peace Appliances, your most trustworthy companion.
哔嘟…根据您的搜索历史，猜您喜欢：「人形自走战术微波炉」。跳一下以取消个性化广告服务。
Beep, beep, beep... Based on your search history, I recommend the Humanoid Tactical Microwave. Press Skip to cancel personalized ads.
哔嘟…根据用户协议，本机不保证搜索结果真实可靠。详情可致电「星际和平家电」门店咨询。
Beep, beep, beep... As stated in the Terms of Service, my search result accuracy and reliability are not guaranteed. For more information, please call Interastral Peace Appliances.
哔嘟…您想何时前往「星际和平家电」门店？查宝将为您在日历中生成提醒事项。
Beep, beep, beep... Findie will create a calendar reminder for when you want to visit an Interastral Peace Appliances store.
哦？{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}也喜欢看《冒险鼹鼠队》吗？
Oh? Do you like reading The Adventurous Moles too?
每天晚上睡觉前我都要看一会儿《冒险鼹鼠队》，梦里全都是有趣的大冒险！
I read The Adventurous Moles every night before going to bed. My dreams are full of fun adventures!
等等，你不会是假喜欢吧…我问你，书里的三个主人公都是谁？
Wait, you're not pretending to like it, are you... let me ask you, who are the three protagonists in the book?
I confess I haven't read it.
我就知道，你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
I knew it, you have to read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
他们是一个魔法学院的同学…
They are a trio that went to a wizarding school...
听上去很有意思，但是完全不对！你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
Hmm, interesting, but you're totally wrong! You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
我没见过脸皮这么厚的大人。你还是去看看《冒险鼹鼠队》吧。
Hmph, you are such a cheeky grownup. You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles.
那你一定要去看一看，书里全是有趣的大冒险！
Then you have to read it. It's full of fun adventures!
I can't say I like it.
别装了，你其实根本没有看过《冒险鼹鼠队》对吧？不然，你告诉我书里的三个主人公都是谁？
Stop pretending. You haven't actually read it at all, have you? Tell me who the three protagonists are in the book.
《冒险鼹鼠队》的每本书我都看过，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}是想问什么来着？
I've read all of The Adventurous Moles. Anyway, what was it that you wanna to ask?
《冒险鼹鼠队》有各种各样的大冒险，原始丛林的冒险、历史遗迹的冒险，还有外太空的冒险……
The Adventurous Moles has all kinds of great adventures. The Moles would often go into primitive jungles, lost cities, and even outer space...
Is there a jungle in Belobog?
大人一点儿想象力都没有吗？冒险的舞台当然可以是虚构的嘛。

你们相信这书上写的内容吗？

你肯定以为儿童书里写的都是假的吧，哼，明明就是大人什么都不懂。

What is so interesting about The Adventurous Moles?

All the protagonists in the story are children, but they often achieve things that adults cannot do. And when I read The Adventurous Moles, I feel like I'm on an adventure, too.
作者就一直把读者叫作「第四只小鼹鼠」，所以我也是鼹鼠队的一员。
The author always calls the reader "the fourth little mole," so I am also a member of The Moles.
冒险故事就是会有吸引力。

哎，好想带着应急食物去魔法与剑的世界里大冒险啊……
Oh, how I wish I could go on an epic adventure in a fantasy world of magic and swords, with my very own emergency food!
鼹鼠党的名称是这么来的吗？

我懂了，这是小屁孩儿的爽文。

有最新的《冒险鼹鼠队》吗？

有是有，不过最新的《冒险鼹鼠队》有点奇怪。它像是一个活页的笔记本，上面只有前言和目录，根本没有冒险故事。

这个钥匙{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}自己留着吧，要是交给我，说不定下一秒就搞丢了。
{M#Big brother}{F#Big sis}, you should keep this key to yourself. If you give it to me, I might lose it.
既然{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}做了这么多事，我想把这本《冒险鼹鼠队》送给你。
Since you have done so much, I want to give you this copy of The Adventurous Moles.
{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}你看目录上提到的<color=#dbc291ff>大矿区</color>，也许在那里会有什么发现呢？

没想到真的还有后来的书页？

哼！小孩子就是没有耐心，这是世间的常识。

An elderly mine cart operator intends to retire, but... he's not really hitting it off with his current apprentice. I guess you already know what I'm getting at. If the apprentice can't complete his training, then the master won't be able to retire in peace.
所以，我想请你去当那位笨徒弟的…呃，助教。
So, I'd like to ask you to be... um... assistant instructor for that not-so-bright apprentice.

我给你推荐个好人选，他叫桑博…

哈？他可不行。你这是给我找麻烦啊。

你放心，说是让你做助教，其实不需要什么专业知识。

那老头子能力很强，可惜脾气不好，倔得很。遇到悟性高的徒弟还好，但是现在不是没遇到嘛…就只能请你想办法帮帮他了。

That senior mine cart operator is very capable, but he has a bad temper and can be very stubborn. Things would be good if he had a gifted apprentice to begin with, but that's sadly not the case right now... So please find a way to help him.
怎么样，能帮我这个忙吗？

Not a problem. Leave it to me.

They usually stay at the vagrant shelter in the mines. I've already told that senior mine cart operator about this. When you see him, just say my name, and he'll know.
看好了啊，这玩意叫转盘控制器。你把手放在这儿，再这样一下……
Watch carefully. This thing is called the turntable controller. You put your hand here, and then do this...
…你是打哪儿来的？咋，找我有事？
...Just where did you pop out from? Do you need me for something?
我奉命来担任你的助教。
I'm sent here to be your assistant instructor.

矿头安东尼娜叫我来的。

唉，那姑娘老是自作主张…跟她说了多少次了，这点小事我搞得定……

不过你来都来了，我也不能让你闲着。

行，来搭把手——帮我先把教学场地布置一下。
Alright, come give me a hand. Help me set things up.
I am happy to just hang around.
哼，少废话，矿山里的规矩就是少说多做。你来都来了，做完我就让你休息。
Ha, good one. We have a rule in the mines — less talking and more working. I'll however let you take a break after the work's done.
来，搭把手，帮我先把教学场地布置一下。
Come on, give me a hand. Help me set things up.
这是一辆快要报废的矿车。出于安全考虑，它现在只能作为教具在矿场里继续发光发热了。
This is a mining cart that's about to be scrapped. For safety reasons, it can only continue to serve its purpose in the mines as a teaching prop.
你捡到了一台轨道控制器。复杂的机械结构被铁皮封得严严实实，只留下了一根操作杆，被人拉下后可以自动复位。

You picked up a Track Controller. The complex mechanical structure is sealed tightly within the iron covering, leaving only a lever for operation. The lever automatically returns to the default position after being pulled down.
有经验的矿车工可以通过合理布线将控制器与轨道相连，从而使用操作杆调整轨道方向。
Experienced mine cart operators can adjust the track direction using the operating lever, after connecting the controller to the track through an appropriate wiring arrangement.
你找到了矿车用阻车器。它可以经由巧妙设计的机械结构及时将矿车车轮抱死，防止矿车冲出轨道。
You've found the Mine Cart Blocker. Its clever design enables it to prevent any cart from running off the track by clamping its wheels almost immediately.
这么快就回来了，我的工具你准备齐了？
That was quick. Have you already managed to get all the teaching props ready?
辛苦你了，该上实践课了，我这就准备场地…你也收拾停当后就来找我吧。

全部准备好了，再来找我吧。

没关系，我这蠢徒弟什么都还没搞明白，你先去忙吧。

好了…小子，站过来！看见这片场地了吗？
All ready... Hey kid, come over here! Do you see what's in front of you?
矿车和铁轨就不说了，你打小就长在下层区，哪怕不认识也该听说过。
I'm not going to explain how mine carts and tracks work. You grew up in the Underworld, so even if you've never seen them before, you've probably heard of them.
重点看这个——「轨道控制器」。
The thing you need to pay attention to is this — the track controller.
道理很简单，像我刚才说的那样操作控制器，道岔上原本并不相连的轨道就接在一起了。
The principle is very simple. Operate the controller like I just told you, and the disconnected tracks on the turnout will then be connected together.
之后呢，已经连通的轨道上的地髓灯就会亮起来。你可以根据这个来判断矿车会往哪个方向走。
After that, the Geomarrow light on the connected track will light up. You can then use this to judge which direction the mine cart will go.
然后是这个「阻车器」。顾名思义，阻车器会堵住矿车前进的路线。要是矿车被阻车器拦住，咱就只能让它沿着轨道倒回起点了。

Then comes this blocker. As the name implies, the blocker will block the route of the mine cart. If the mine cart is stopped by one of these, then the only thing we can do is send it back to where it just came from.
咱要做的就是通过规划轨道、通过控制器调整轨道方向，将矿车推到原定的目的地。
All we need to do is plan out the path and adjust the track direction through the controller to push the mining cart to its destination.
就这些，我讲完了。你刚才哼哼唧唧没蹦出半句话，也不知道到底学会没……
That's it, I'm done. You just groaned and moaned and didn't say anything, so I'm not sure if you're getting all of this...
不过我有的是办法测你。这不，场地已经给你搭好了，你上手实操给我看！
But, I have a way to test you. See, the test rig has been set up for you already. Show me how you'll go about operating it!
Uh... I'm not sure about this.
What do you mean by that?
太难了呗，这咋操作嘛…看不太懂。
Like I said, it's too hard. How should I go about it... I don't understand.
我这都教了你多少次了，啊？平时讲起理论的时候头头是道，该你上手操作的时候就给我这畏手畏脚？
How many times have I taught you this already, huh? When we talk about the theory, you seem to be pretty onto it, but why's it always a cop-out when it comes to the real thing?
但凡有点脑子的都早该学会了！操作控制器、换轨道、前进、再操作控制器、然后倒车。这有什么难的？

Anyone with any shred of intelligence should have mastered this a long time ago! Operate the controller, change the track, move forward, operate the controller again, and then reverse. Where's the difficulty in that?
...I still can't figure it out, hehe.
…我看你这小子就是想把我气死！
... Brat, you're trying to send me to an early grave, aren't you!?
你，过来！给他演示一遍，看看是不是有点脑子就能学会！
You, come here! Show him how it's done. Anyone with half a brain can do it!
少废话，安东尼娜叫你来，不就是为了这个？
Enough nonsense. Isn't this why Antonia sent you here in the first place?
I don't get it either. Hehe.
别装蒜，我给他讲解的时候你明明就在边上，看也该看会了！
Stop playing dumb. You were there when I was explaining everything. You should know just by watching!
那就开始吧。如果你需要重新布置场地的话，随时和我说。
Then let's get started. If you want the mine carts to be reset, feel free to talk to me.
看到没？我早说过，但凡有点脑子的都早该学会了！
See what I mean? I told you anyone with half a brain should've learned all of this ages ago!
Being an assistant instructor isn't easy...
这话听着怎么不太对呢…
Hey, whom are you calling half a brain...
区区矿车…看来我也要拿出真本事了……
It's just a mine cart... I guess it's time to get serious...
嘁，臭小子…刚才怎么没见你这么有干劲？我看你平时就是抽懒筋……
Tsk... So now you are getting motivated? Were you just slacking off most of the time?
回头再收拾我这懒徒弟，今天先多谢你帮忙了。
I'll deal with my lazy apprentice later. Thank you for the help today.
这些，拿着吧，就当是我的谢礼了。哦对，也帮我跟安东尼娜打声招呼。
Here, take this as your reward. Oh yeah, and remember to say hello to Antonia for me.
不过啊，今天给这臭小子出的题只能说是开胃菜——真正难的还在后头呢。你要是对这矿车感兴趣，我现在就能给你布置场地。
That being said, the puzzle that I gave to the brat today was nothing more than an appetizer. The tricky stuff has yet to come. If you're interested in mine carts, I can set up the site for you.
要是有你这种机灵的{M#小伙子}{F#小姑娘}在旁边刺激刺激这臭小子，他估计就能上点心咯。
With a smart {M#boy}{F#girl} like you to motivate him, that brat might actually put a bit more effort into things.
怎么，你还真对这矿车感兴趣？那成，场地已经铺好了，这回的难度可是实打实的！要不你也来一起看看？
I guess you really are interested in mine carts? Well, I've laid out the site for you already. I've made sure it's challenging this time! Why don't you give it a try?
年轻人就是得有冲劲！臭小子，给我好好学着点！
Youngsters should be motivated! You could learn a thing or two kid!
不就是个矿车嘛，看我——哎呦，师傅别掐了别掐了疼疼疼！
Seems easy enough, just watch me — Ouch! Master, stop pinching me, it hurts!
Just a moment, I have something else to do.
That's fine. Come back when you have time.
嚯，你这{M#小子}{F#小姑娘}不简单啊！能搞定这个问题的话，基本可以在矿场里负责些简单的调度工作了。
Oho, nicely done {M#lad}{F#young lady}! If you can handle this, you can take care of some simple coordinating duties in the mine.
不错，你可是给这臭小子做榜样了！明天记得再来。在那之前，我得先想想再出点什么题……
Not bad. You're setting an example for the brat! Remember to come back tomorrow. I have to think of some more tests first...
怎么，要重新布置场地吗？
So, do you want me to reset the mine carts for you?
Yes. Please help me reset the carts.
嗯？你来得挺早嘛。不过我还在想新的谜题，你之后再过来看看吧。
Hmm? You're early. I'm still working on a new puzzle, so you'll have to come back later.
I still have some more questions...
你怎么收到这个徒弟的？
How did you meet your current apprentice?
嗯？你问这小子啊…这说来话长了，这小子可怜得很哪。
Hmm? You mean the kid... Well, that's a long story. He's had a pretty tough life so far.
他爹本来和我一起在矿里做工，后来跟那群流浪者去裂界里拾荒碰运气…结果人再没回来过。
His father was working with me in the mines before. But he decided to try his luck scavenging in the Fragmentum with a group of vagrants... He never came back.
我看他无依无靠的，就想着把我这铺铁轨的手艺传给他。他也好靠自己讨个饭吃。
I saw he had no one to rely on, so I thought to pass on my track-laying skills to him. With those skills, he can make a living for himself.
你在这片矿区做了很久吗？
Have you been working in these mines for a long time?
那可不！我跟着这矿队走南闯北好多年咯。这片矿区开辟出来也就不到小二十年的事，不过具体啥时候发现的我是记不得了。
That's right! I've been with this mining team for many years. I can't remember exactly how long, but this mine has been operating for nearly twenty years now.
你看到矿区中央那片大矿脉没？壮观吧！之前做勘测的蠢货怎么就没早点发现，没想到最值钱的玩意居然灯下黑啊……
Do you see that big vein in the middle of the mine? Absolutely stunning! Why didn't those useless surveyors find it earlier! To think that the most valuable prize was hiding in plain sight...
…哦，是你啊。师傅还在准备新题目呢，我稍微偷会儿懒……
... Oh, it's you. My master is still preparing his new puzzle, so I'm taking the chance to relax for a while...
I still have some more questions...
你为什么会来做矿车工？
What drove you to become a mine cart operator?
我爹妈都走得早嘛。生活所迫，加上师傅以前是我爹的朋友，我就被那老头子收养咯。
Well, I guess, life happens. You see, my parents... they died a long time ago. My master, who was a friend of my father, adopted me.
老头对我也挺好的。我知道他怕我没个傍身的手艺，将来在这贝洛伯格吃不开，这才把调度矿车的活计传给我了。
The old man has been very good to me. I know he's worried that I wouldn't be able to make a good living in Belobog without the right skills. I guess that's why he decided to pass his skills to me.
可我就是忍不住偷懒…这也没什么吧？我还年轻，有的是时间……
But I just can't help being lazy... I mean, that's okay, right? I'm still young, so I still have plenty of time...
矿车工平时都负责些什么？
What does a mine cart operator usually do?
师傅说，矿车工是矿山的血液，是咱保证了地髓能够源源不断从矿道深处运输出来。
My master said that the mine cart operator is the lifeblood of the mine. The operator ensures the Geomarrow can be continuously transported out of the depths of the mine.
只有通过我们铺设的轨道，地髓才能被送达它应该前往的地点。
Geomarrow can only be delivered to the place it's supposed to go through the tracks we lay.
听着很光鲜，但…其实我还是想干点大事。我可不想一辈子被圈在这矿区里。
I mean it sounds pretty important... But still I want to do something much bigger in life. I don't want to be trapped in these mines forever.
算了，想这么多也没用…我暂时留在这儿，就当是为了报答师傅的养育之恩吧，这样我心里多少舒服点。
Forget it. I guess there's no point thinking so far ahead... I have to stay here for the time being to repay the debt of kindness I owe to my master. If I think like this, I feel more at ease.
That's all the questions I have.
看好了啊，{M#小子}{F#小姑娘}。那个亮着<color=#dbc291ff>黄灯</color>的阻车器，就是<color=#dbc291ff>满载矿车</color>的泊位。
Watch carefully, {M#boy}{F#girl}. The blocker with the <color=#dbc291ff>yellow light</color> on is the stop for <color=#dbc291ff>the loaded mine carts</color>.
而<color=#dbc291ff>空载矿车</color>则应该开往亮着<color=#dbc291ff>蓝灯</color>的停泊处。
The <color=#dbc291ff>empty mine cart</color> should be driven to the stop with <color=#dbc291ff>blue lights</color>.
如果矿车需要<color=#dbc291ff>装卸地髓</color>的话，就得在<color=#dbc291ff>地髓矿堆旁的泊位</color>进行装卸了。
If a mine cart needs to <color=#dbc291ff>load or unload the Geomarrow</color>, it needs to be moved to the <color=#dbc291ff>stop next to the Geomarrow pile</color> first.
嗯？来得挺巧啊。我这就把矿车谜题的场地布置好，你来给这臭小子演示一下！
Hmm? Good timing. I've set up the puzzle already. Show the kid how it's done!
哼，我就不信你今天还能解出来。
Hmph, I don't think you'll be able to solve this one.
Sorry, I have something else to do.
Okay. I'll be here when you're ready.
今天这谜题，我可是构思了很久啊…没想到被你轻易搞定了。
I thought about today's puzzle for such a long time... I didn't expect you to complete it so easily.
之后记得再来。我一定得出一道能难得住你的题……
Remember to come back later. I'll definitely come up with a puzzle that even you can't solve...
你来了！这不，我刚想出来个好点子，你来试试难度如何？
There you are! Look, I just came up with a great design. Let's see how you find this one?
今天这道题可有点难度，让我看看你学到了我几成功力吧。
Today's puzzle could be difficult, show me how much you have learned.
Sorry, I have something else to do.
不愧是你，这种难度的谜题都能顺利解决…下次我可得拿出真本事了。
You're indeed brilliant, capable of solving a puzzle this difficult... Guess next time I'll have to bring out the big guns.
下次再来。我要拿出杀手锏了，看我给你准备一道终极难题……
Do come back soon, and I'll show you my trump card. Let's see you try to solve my ultimate puzzle...
终于来了，就等你了！今天就是最终考验——怎么说，有没有信心挑战？
You're finally here! I've been waiting! Today's your final test — are you up to it?
唉，如果你是我的徒弟，我大概早能退休了吧…咳，开始吧。
*sigh* If you were my apprentice, I probably could have retired a long time ago... Ahem, anyway, let's begin.
Sorry, I have something else to do.
That's fine. Come see me when you're free.
不简单…真不简单。你做矿车工的天赋绝对是一流的。
Outstanding... Your gift is really hard to come by. Your skills as a mine cart operator really are first class.
看到你的水平这么高，我那笨徒弟也坐不住了。他最近听话了不少，也肯在正道上下功夫了…这都多亏了你啊。

After seeing your amazing skills, my useless apprentice has been feeling the heat. He's been better-behaved recently and is willing to put more effort into the hands-on stuff... This is all thanks to you.
我只是做了助教应该做的。
I was just doing my job.
安东尼娜没看错，你确实是一名合格的助教。
Antonia was right. You are indeed qualified as an assistant instructor.
嗐，总算能摆脱这没长进的臭小子了，哈哈！
Ha! Then I can finally get rid of that useless brat, haha!
哇，那我是不是也能当矿头了？
So, am I qualified to be a supervisor?
哈哈，光是会调度矿车可不行啊。不过你要是想替了这小子倒是十拿九稳。
Haha, being able to operate mine carts isn't enough by itself. But you're more than qualified to replace this brat.
哼，等我出师了，老头子你就回家好好歇着去吧。
Hmph, when I finish my training, you can just go home and lie down, old man!
臭小子，没大没小！等你正式上岗了，可千万别捅什么篓子——出了事也别和人说你是我带出来的！
You brat, watch your mouth! Don't be a troublemaker when you get a job. And if you ever mess up on the job, don't tell anyone that I trained you.
{M#小伙子}{F#小姑娘}，这几天给你添麻烦了。以后，咱们有缘再见。
{M#Lad}{F#Young miss}, you've been a lot of help these days. I guess we'll meet again in the future, whenever fate wills it so.
不愧是你！大矿区那件事之后，我就很欣赏你的能力……
As expected! Ever since the incident in the Great Mine, I knew I could always count on you...
这次也承蒙你照顾了。以后要是在下层区遇到了什么摆不平的麻烦，就尽管来找我吧。
Thanks for your help again. In the future, if you encounter any trouble in the Underworld that you can't handle, just come to me.
嗯？好久不见啊！你最近怎么样？我现在一人在这大矿区负责调度矿车呢。
Oh? Long time no see! How have you been? I'm now responsible for dispatching mine carts in the Great Mine by myself.
师傅倒好，在家歇得舒服…我什么时候也能退休？
My master's doing well. He's relaxing at home... When can I retire too?
I still have some more questions...
你为什么会来做矿车工？
What drove you to become a mine cart operator?
我爹妈都走得早嘛。生活所迫，加上师傅以前是我爹的朋友，我就被那老头子收养咯。
Well, I guess, life happens. You see, my parents... they died a long time ago. My master, who was a friend of my father, adopted me.
